feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: GRC IAG Bridge: SF RepSync does not show role imported in BRM
entry.content:
Hi All,

GRC 12, SP20

Although rep sync job from GRC shows as successful, GRACROLE does not show the static group imported. However, GRACRLCONN does. The IAG role import was tick marked during the rep sync job. 

FYI, my SF connector is maintained in IAG_GRP Connector type.

Can you please suggest on this. And also the class/interface required to maintain in Integration Scenario ROLMG for Connector type IAG_GRP

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Common Master data and Master data for access control, access analysis in IAG
entry.content: I am implementing IAG for one of my clients. I have most of the configuration but am stuck at maintenance of common master data and master data separately for access analysis, access control and PAM. I am not sure where do I have to maintain that data. Any help will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: How to use 2 IAS tenants in single GRC-IAG bridge for access provisioning in Nonprod and prod system
entry.content:
Is it possible to use 2 different IAS (Non-Prod & Prod) tenants in single GRC-IAG bridge setup.

If yes, how GRC system will identify which IAS to check if single GRC-IAG Bridge system is used for access provisioning to both Non-Prod or Production cloud systems as both cloud systems will have different IAS tenants as source for user data.

entry.title: SAP Application User provision based on Application Role via Entra ID
entry.content:
Hi Team,

As SAP IDM is expected to retire soon in the coming years, wanted to know possibility of it being replaced by Entra ID ( azure AD).

SAP IDM and SAP applications including security components - SAP GRC and IAG are very well integrated with each other as they are a part of same family.

Wanted to know if Entra ID can replace SAP IDM , with respect to tasks like :

1. Informing SAP IPS system to provision users to target application based on approval/deny event from SAP GRC or SAP IAG.

Is this possible with Entra Id ? How does the SAP Application role information flow from SAP GRC / IAG to Entra ID is it even possible ?

entry.title: TRM(Treasury management ) risks and functions for SAP IAG or GRC
entry.content: Hi Guys, can anyone share TRM(Treasury management ) risks and functions for SAP IAG or GRC.I have to implement it but have not idea. I saw a post by madhusap by he didn't give the risks or functions. Would really appreciate any help

entry.title: Business Role creation in SAP IAG or SAP GRC in Hybrid approach?
entry.content:
Hello all,

We will be using GRC AC and IAG Bridge to extend the current GRC functionality for Access Management.
There will be Hybrid approach where Access request will be provisioned through GRC if its for On-premise system and through IAG if its for Cloud Apps. Initial access request will be triggered from GRC only.

I would like to know what will be feasible-

1. Creating Business roles in IAG for cloud apps and then sync it with GRC? in GRC job roles for On-premise system are already available. So create another Business role in GRC which will contain Job role of On-premise and Business role from IAG.

or

2. Create a Business role in IAG for cloud apps and include all the job-roles from On-prem in the same business role via sync. Is this possible in IAG bridge scenario?

Let me know if any further information is required.

Thank you.

entry.title: Seeking Guidance on Enabling SSO for SAP BTP UI5 CAPM Application with S/4 HANA Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm new to the SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition field and eager to learn as much as possible. I need your expertise on a specific scenario: enabling Single Sign-On (SSO) for my SAP BTP UI5 CAPM application hosted on Cloud Foundry, integrated with S/4 HANA Public Edition via Cloud Identity Services.

Article 1

Article 2

Article 3

I've come across various articles on this topic, but they mostly focus on on-premise S/4 HANA and BTP applications. If you have any articles, step-by-step guides, or video tutorials, I would greatly appreciate it if you could share them.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,
Rahul Jain

entry.title: Seeking Advice on Custom Authentication for CAPM Services with HANA Cloud Database
entry.content:
Good day to everyone,

I'm new to the CAPM world and have recently started learning its concepts. We've developed CAP services and now need to secure them by implementing authorization and authentication.

SAP provides XSUAA, which ensures that only authorized users can access endpoints by establishing a trusted connection with identity providers for user authentication. This concept works well from SAP's perspective.

However, in our scenario, we have developed CAPM services that are bound to a HANA Cloud Database (HDI Containers). Our database/schema contains a users table (ID, Name, Username, Password). We want to implement a system where authorization and authentication occur only if the user exists in our user table.

Instead of authenticating users against identity providers, I want to validate users against the records in our tables and generate a token if the records exist.

Is this approach correct? In ASP.NET Web API, we usually follow this practice.

Looking forward to your suggestions.

Best regards,
Rahul Jain

entry.title: IPS Sync Issue : S/4HANA Cloud Private edition User and Roles Sync
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

We're trying to sync the users and their roles from SAP S/4Hana Cloud, Private Edition to Work Zone (standard) using IPS sync. 

We've followed the below process by referring the help documents. 

  • Configure the Identity Provisioning Service | SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Application Server ABAP | SAP Help Portal
  • Configure Identity Provisioning Service | SAP Help Portal

However, while performing the sync we're getting issue "Initialization of repository destination <destination_name> Failed: No user identity is configured". 

S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition version : 2022 

Steps Followed: 

1. Established the cloud connector setup with BTP Subaccount 

2. Subscribed the SAP Identity service, connectivity plan to BTP subaccount based on IAS region

3. Created a dedicated destination with "RFC" as connection type with cloud connector information and checked the connection 

4. Created Source system with type "SAP Application Server ABAP" and selected the destination (point3).

5. Created target system with type "SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition" 

6. Performed the Read / Resync action from the Source system. 

 

Thanks.

entry.title: IAS/ SSO login for user with two different SF username but same email
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We want to introduce different login method's for Admins vs  normal Users.

Current scenario : Example User John is an admin as well as an employee he has however one email address maintained in SF and two user id's a_John & P_John . He  uses  the same SF url to login into both accounts (At this stage SSO is not enabled ) and he use's userid to login .

in Future : Client will upgrade to use IAS / IPS . However in IAS unique email address is needed  hence only one account is synced with the email in IAS .

User wants a way to login into the SF system in which SSO is enabled such that he can access both his accounts via the same email . Login methods could be different for an admin id vs he as normal employee / user .

Please suggest how we can achieve this. Can we have conditional login for admins such that they have a choice to enter SF either as a admin or employee ? or can we have SSO enabled for user as a employee and disable for admins ? Please suggest Whats  are the possibilities & solutions.

Thanks,

Zeba

entry.title: How to manage Mitigation Control assignment Approval workflow in GRC-IAG Bridge ?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are working with customer and have the below scenario encountered, Please help us understand what would be the appropriate way to move forward in this IAG-GRC AC Bridge scenario :

Scenario : Customer will have bridge scenario of GRC-IAG, Access request feature will be utilized from GRC AC Access request form for cloud and on-premise systems ( AS-IS : Mitigation Control approval workflow is currently in activate state for GRC AC which triggers on assignment of Mitigation Control to owner for approval during access request in case of On-premise systems)

Note : Access Control Risk Definition synchronization job & Access Control Mitigation Control Transfer job are instructed to run only once for syncing data from GRC AC to IAG to fetch data and recommended to start the maintenance of Risk and Mitigation data for both On-Premise & Cloud IAG moving forward in IAG.

Question : How to manage mitigation control assignment workflow for Cloud and On-Premise risk in GRC AC Access Request Form if mitigation data would be coming from IAG as runtime for cloud and on-premise systems, Customer wants to continue with Mitigation control approval workflow which is critical for business.

Regards

Asit Kumar Behera

entry.title: GRC integration with OKTA for user data source
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a Okta server where our client store user information, whenever any user join's the organization his/her data is stored in okta and samaccount/email is created for users.

As you must be knowing OKTA is a identity solution where user details are stored, we want to use OKTA as users data source.

We want to understand if GRC support OKTA tool as a part of integration or not.

If GRC doesn't support OKTA then can we integrate OKTA with IAG using IAS/IPS then we can use IAG as a bridge with GRC where we want user's data along with manger/supervisor.

We are also using OKTA as Corporate Idp via SAP IAS. for SSO for all system like SAC, IBP, S4, BTP etc

Please provide the solution for above scenarios how we can integrate OKTA with GRC or IAG.

Regards,
Surya Pratap Singh

entry.title: Enable SAP IAG on SAP mobile start application
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can we enable SAP IAG in the SAP Mobile Start application?

Could someone please assist with the process or provide any relevant documentation?

Thanks

Prameel

entry.title: Custom tile & catalog in S/4HANA Public Cloud (2SL-SB)
entry.content:
A customer on the S/4HANA Public Cloud 2SL - Solution Builder would like to know whether it is possible somehow to have a custom tile in S/4 with a custom catalog which could take the user to SAP IAG home screen or somehow offer this x-app navigation to new hires (without paying for S/4 licence since there are no S/4 apps used at this point). Technically after a user is authorised by IAG then the user gets an SAP role (which are licensed). 

This seems to be possible in 3SL landscape using a custom catalog, but in 2SL it is not possible to create a  custom catalog. See this blog.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Identity Federation in IAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured Azure and IAS as a proxy. Users and groups from Azure are provisioned to IAS and granted access in BTP. However, there appears to be an issue with the identity federation.

Settings in IAS:

  • Allow Identity Authentication users only = OFF
  • Identity Federation: Use Identity Authentication user store = ON
  • Default Name ID Format =  unspecified
  • Subject Name Identifier = ${corporateIdP.userPrincipalName}
  • Attributes:
    • Corporate Identity Provider = ${corporateIdP.Groups}
    • Corporate Identity Provider = ${mail}
    • Corporate Identity Provider = ${login_name}
    •  

When a user exists in Azure but is not present in IAS, they cannot log in to the SAP Cloud App.

The following error message appears:

Error: Due to a misconfiguration, the Identity Provider cannot process the response. Please contact your system administrator. Troubleshooting: You can provide your administrator with the following correlation ID:

What do you think might be causing this issue ?

Thank you for your help.

Best regards

entry.title: IAS as central user management system
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

If I understand correctly, it is recommended to use the IAS user store as the central user management system.

I have the following question for clarification:

I have configured Azure as the Idp and the IAS as a proxy. Using the IPS, the groups from Azure are provisioned to IAS. If I, for example, delete/modify a user in IAS, that user is re-provisioned to the IAS user store during the next IPS job. Or, for example, the users were still existing as shadow users in the BTP subaccounts, even though they were deleted in Azure AD

How is central user management in IAS configured?

Is this the recommended approach?

Many Thanks

entry.title: Why Identity Access Governance is not available/found in SAP Discovery Center?
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

For some reason, I am unable to search for Identity Access Governance in SAP Discovery Center? Why? 

 

 

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Provision users in sac
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have two group in IAS: one group (sac_azure) provision from Azure and onw group (sac_ias) creted in IAS direkt.

How can I provision only this two groups to SAC? I have configured this  properties „sac.group.filter = displayName eq "sac_azure","sac_ias"   in the IAS Source System, But still, all users are provisioned to SAC.

Thanks in advance

Best Regards

entry.title: S4 HANA Public Cloud: Cost Center Responsible Report
entry.content:
Hello all,

We have the following user story

“As a cost center manager, I should be able to see cost center plan/actual values only for my responsible cost center”.

We have already tried to work with individual roles, which are explicitly limited to one cost center. Here, however, we have the problem that other roles that the cost center manager has, which are more comprehensive in the area of cost centers, cancel each other out.

Do any of you know of another way, add-on or similar?

entry.title: SAP IPS Read/Resync Job
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I'm still not entirely clear on this.

Specifically, when I make changes to user properties (e.g., the username has changed due to marriage) in Azure and then run the "READ" job in IAS, these changes are not provisioned to IAS. Only changes like creating a new group or user in Azure are provisioned to IAS.

However, when I run the "RESYNC" job, the new changes are transferred to IAS, but all attributes (including those I modified in IAS, as well as custom attributes) get overwritten.

How do you manage this?

Which jobs should be executed to ensure that the additional attributes I entered in IAS are not overwritten?

How can we provision only the changes from Azure?

How can we maintain attributes in IAS so that they are not overwritten during the next job?

Many Thanks for support and tips!

Best Regards
